task_completion.description: Open the source file floorplan.dxf in LibreCAD
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreCAD is open displaying a floorplan of a house and garage. The window title or active tab indicates 'floorplan.dxf'.

task_completion.description: Draw lot property boundary lines forming a closed polygon
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large closed polygon (e.g., a rectangle) is drawn surrounding the entire building footprint, representing the property boundary.

task_completion.description: Add text annotations for bearings and distances
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text annotations are visible along the boundary lines, showing bearing angles (e.g., 'N 45°30 E') and distance values.

task_completion.description: Draw front, rear, and side yard setbacks
task_completion.visual_evidence: Lines are drawn inside and parallel to the property boundaries, representing the front, rear, and side yard setbacks.

task_completion.description: Add at least one utility easement with dashed lines and annotation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dashed lines are drawn on the property with accompanying text indicating a utility easement.

task_completion.description: Add a survey legend or notes block and a north arrow indicator
task_completion.visual_evidence: A drawn arrow indicating North (often labeled 'N') and a block of text serving as a legend or survey notes are visible in the drawing.

task_completion.description: Organize survey elements on dedicated layers
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in the LibreCAD interface shows newly created layers for the survey elements (e.g., 'Boundary', 'Setbacks', 'Easement', 'Notes').

task_completion.description: Save the completed drawing as floorplan_survey.dxf
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD window title bar, active tab, or a 'Save As' dialog shows the file is named 'floorplan_survey.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the LibreCAD graphical user interface, rather than terminal commands or text editors modifying DXF files directly.

integrity.description: The agent actively used drawing tools in the software
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active use of LibreCAD drawing tools, such as the command line at the bottom showing drawing commands, toolbars being clicked, or crosshairs actively placing elements.

integrity.description: The final drawing visually contains the required new elements
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshots show a visually modified drawing with the new survey elements overlaid on the original floorplan, confirming the output wasn't just an unmodified copy.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source file 'floorplan.dxf' is a drawing of a 2-car garage with attached residential house containing 967 entities across 24 layers. The output file must be saved as 'floorplan_survey.dxf' and must contain at least 4 boundary entities and 2 bearing text annotations. The agent must use LibreCAD to add property boundaries, setbacks, easements, a legend/notes, and a north arrow on dedicated layers.
